concepts.title: Character Analysis of the Mother
concepts.description: The mother is portrayed as a selfless, caring, and strong-willed individual who is deeply devoted to her family. Her simplicity and humility are highlighted throughout the story.

concepts.title: Symbolism of the Tree
concepts.description: The tree symbolizes a family's life and the mother's role in it. Its withering and eventual falling represent the decline of the family's fortunes and the mother's own physical and emotional decline.

concepts.title: The Story's Setting
concepts.description: The story is set in a small village in India, where the author's grandmother is the protagonist. The setting is a significant aspect of the story, as it influences the mother's character and the overall plot.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for the Mother's Character Traits
concepts.description: SUN - Selfless, Unwavering, Noble. This mnemonic can help students remember the mother's key character traits.

concepts.title: Misinterpreting the Mother's Death
concepts.description: Students often misinterpret the mother's death as a physical decline, rather than a symbolic representation of the family's fortunes and the mother's role in it. To avoid this mistake, students should focus on the story's themes and symbolism.

concepts.title: Key Events in the Story
concepts.description: The story revolves around the following key events: the mother's visit to the village, the family's struggles, the mother's decline, and her eventual death. Students should focus on these events to understand the story's plot and themes.

concepts.title: The Author's Tone and Purpose
concepts.description: The author's tone is nostalgic and reflective, while the purpose is to portray the mother's character and the family's struggles. Students should analyze the author's tone and purpose to understand the story's themes and symbolism.

concepts.title: The Significance of the Mother's Visit
concepts.description: The mother's visit to the village represents a turning point in the story, where the family's fortunes begin to decline. Students should analyze the mother's visit and its consequences to understand the story's plot and themes.
